inputs.label: Lawn Area
inputs.businessContext: Total area of lawn to be maintained. Standard measure for landscaping cost estimation (ISO 14000, landscaping industry benchmarks).

inputs.label: Service Frequency
inputs.businessContext: Frequency of lawn care service. Affects total annual cost. Common industry practice (APICS, landscaping service contracts).

inputs.label: Labor Rate
inputs.businessContext: Hourly wage for lawn care workers. Based on local minimum wage and market rates (Bureau of Labor Statistics).

inputs.label: Equipment Cost
inputs.businessContext: Annual cost of equipment (mowers, trimmers, etc.) including depreciation and maintenance. Industry standard for small landscaping businesses (Lean, Six Sigma cost analysis).

inputs.label: Material Cost
inputs.businessContext: Annual cost of fertilizers, pesticides, seeds, etc. Based on typical lawn care product usage (ISO 14000 environmental management).

inputs.label: Overhead Rate
inputs.businessContext: Percentage of direct costs allocated to overhead (administration, insurance, etc.). Standard cost accounting practice (Maynard Industrial Engineering Handbook).

inputs.label: Data Confidence
inputs.businessContext: Confidence level in input data. Affects adjustment of final cost estimate. Common in risk analysis (Six Sigma DMAIC).

rules: lawnArea must be between 10 and 100000 m²
If serviceFrequency is 'weekly', then annual visits = 52; if 'biweekly', 26; if 'monthly', 12

thresholds.laborRate: If laborRate > 50, warning: 'High labor rate may indicate premium service'
thresholds.overheadRate: If overheadRate > 30, warning: 'Overhead rate exceeds typical range'
thresholds.equipmentCost: If equipmentCost > 5000, warning: 'Equipment cost is high; consider leasing'

suggestedActions: Optimize service frequency based on grass growth rate
Consider bulk purchasing of materials to reduce materialCost
Evaluate equipment leasing vs. buying to lower equipmentCost
Review overhead allocation for potential savings
